title: Trymalium westringiifolium
taxonomy: Trymalium westringiifolium is classified within the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the genus Trymalium, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. The species name westringiifolium refers to the similarity of its leaves to those of the genus Westringia. Trymalium westringiifolium is a shrub that is endemic to the eastern regions of Australia. The classification of this species is further supported by its characteristics and morphology, which are typical of the Rhamnaceae family.

Studies have shown that Trymalium westringiifolium possesses several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments.
